---
title: "Simulation_processing"
output: pdf_document
date: "2025-11-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(parallel)
library(viridis)
library(latex2exp)
source(file.path(Sys.getenv("LEPTO_BURDEN_ROOT", "."), "R", "paths.R"))

#######################################Convergence################################################

file_list <- list.files(
  simulation_path("Simulation_output"),
  pattern = "*.rds",
  full.names = TRUE
)

num_cores <- detectCores()
set <- 1:length(file_list)
```

## Convergence

```{r conv}
# 2. Nested Extraction: File -> List of Models -> Rhats & Checks
results_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract file-level parameters
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  job  <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=job_)\\d+"))
  
  # Read the list of lists
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  # INNER LOOP: Iterate through every simulation model
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(single_model) {
    
    # Robust check: ensure the model has rhats (skip if NULL/failed sim)
    if (is.null(single_model) || is.null(single_model$rhat)) return(NULL)
    
    rhat_vals <- single_model$rhat
    es_val <- single_model$es_2024
    
    # Check if ES is valid (not NA)
    # We use isTRUE to safely handle cases where es_val might be NULL or logical(0)
    es_is_valid <- (!is.na(es_val) & es_val<100000)
    
    tibble(
      sdalpha0 = sdalpha0,
      piP = piP,
      job = job,
      
      # Definition: All Rhats < 1.1 AND es_2024 is not NA
      converged_1_1 = all(rhat_vals < 1.1, na.rm = TRUE) & es_is_valid,
      
      # Definition: All Rhats < 1.01 AND es_2024 is not NA
      converged_1_01 = all(rhat_vals < 1.01, na.rm = TRUE) & es_is_valid
    )
  })
  
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# 3. Combine and Summarize
summary_df <- results_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%                           
  group_by(sdalpha0,piP) %>%                    
  summarise(
    total_sims = n(),                       
    percent_less_1_1 = sum(converged_1_1, na.rm = TRUE)/200 * 100,
    percent_less_1_01 = sum(converged_1_01, na.rm = TRUE)/200 * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )


ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = sdalpha0, y = percent_less_1_1, color = factor(piP), group = piP)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = TeX("SD of normal prior for logit$(\\alpha_0)$"),
    y = "Percent Converged (Rhat < 1.1)",
    color = TeX("logit$^{-1}(\\alpha_0)$")
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Forecasting

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r forecast, echo=FALSE}

# --- 1. PARALLEL PROCESSING ---
results_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    
    if (is.null(sim) || is.null(sim$rhat)) return(NULL)
    if (any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    
    tibble(
      sdalpha0 = sdalpha0,
      piP = piP,
      es_2024 = sim$es_2024,
      crps = sim$crps,
      bias_2024 = sim$bias_2024,
      rate_2024_cover = sim$rate_2024_cover
    )
  })
  
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# --- 2. AGGREGATION ---
summary_df <- results_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(sdalpha0, piP) %>%
  summarise(
    median_es = median(es_2024, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_crps = median(crps, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_percent_bias = (exp(mean(bias_2024, na.rm = TRUE)) - 1) * 100,
    mean_coverage = mean(rate_2024_cover, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_converged = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- 3. SPLIT DATA FOR CONTROLLED PLOTTING ---
df_main <- summary_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(median_es, median_crps, mean_percent_bias),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    "median_es" = "Median ES",
    "median_crps" = "Median CRPS",
    "mean_percent_bias" = "Mean Percent Bias"
  ))

df_cov <- summary_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = mean_coverage,
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(metric = "Mean Coverage")

# Reference lines for specific facets
ref_lines <- tibble(
  metric = c("Mean Coverage", "Mean Percent Bias"),
  yintercept = c(95, 0)
)

# --- 4. PLOT ---
ggplot() +
  # Main metrics
  geom_line(
    data = df_main,
    aes(x = sdalpha0, y = value, color = factor(piP)),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_main,
    aes(x = sdalpha0, y = value, color = factor(piP)),
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Coverage
  geom_line(
    data = df_cov,
    aes(x = sdalpha0, y = value, color = factor(piP)),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_cov,
    aes(x = sdalpha0, y = value, color = factor(piP)),
    size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Reference lines
  geom_hline(
    data = ref_lines,
    aes(yintercept = yintercept),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +  # 2x2 layout
  
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0.5,1.0,1.5)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = TeX("logit$^{-1}(\\alpha_0)$")) +
  
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = TeX("SD of normal prior for logit$(\\alpha_0)$"),
    y = "Estimate Value"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )
```

## Parameter evaluation by region

```{r param_region}

extracted_metrics_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract parameters
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    # --- EXCLUSIONS ---
    if (is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    # 1. Convergence (Rhat > 1.1)
    if (is.null(sim$rhat) || any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    # 2. ES Validity (NA)
    if (is.null(sim$es_2024) || is.na(sim$es_2024)) return(NULL)
    # 3. Dataframe existence
    if (is.null(sim$param_metrics) || !is.data.frame(sim$param_metrics)) return(NULL)
    
    # --- EXTRACTION ---
    sim$param_metrics %>%
      dplyr::select(region, log_bias_rate, prob_bias, abs_err_rate, abs_err_prob, cover_rate, cover_prob) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = -region, names_to = "metric_type", values_to = "value") %>%
      mutate(sdalpha0 = sdalpha0, piP = piP)
  })
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# --- 2. AGGREGATION AND TRANSFORMATION ---

final_summary_df <- extracted_metrics_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(sdalpha0, piP, region, metric_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    # Transformations
    transformed_value = case_when(
      metric_type == "log_bias_rate" ~ (exp(avg_value) - 1) * 100,
      metric_type %in% c("abs_err_rate", "abs_err_prob") ~ avg_value,
      TRUE ~ avg_value
    ),
    # Clean labels
    display_metric = case_when(
      metric_type == "log_bias_rate" ~ "Percent Bias (Rate)",
      metric_type == "prob_bias" ~ "Bias (Prob)",
      metric_type == "abs_err_rate" ~ "MAE (Rate)",
      metric_type == "abs_err_prob" ~ "MAE (Prob)",
      metric_type == "cover_rate" ~ "Coverage (Rate)",
      metric_type == "cover_prob" ~ "Coverage (Prob)",
      TRUE ~ metric_type
    )
  )

extracted_metrics_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract parameters
  nh <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=nh_)\\d+"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    # --- EXCLUSIONS ---
    if (is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    # 1. Convergence (Rhat > 1.1)
    if (is.null(sim$rhat) || any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    # 2. ES Validity (NA)
    if (is.null(sim$es_2024) || is.na(sim$es_2024)) return(NULL)
    # 3. Dataframe existence
    if (is.null(sim$param_metrics) || !is.data.frame(sim$param_metrics)) return(NULL)
    
    # --- EXTRACTION ---
    sim$param_metrics %>%
      dplyr::select(region, log_bias_rate, prob_bias, abs_err_rate, abs_err_prob, cover_rate, cover_prob) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = -region, names_to = "metric_type", values_to = "value") %>%
      mutate(nh = nh, piP = piP)
  })
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# --- 2. AGGREGATION AND TRANSFORMATION ---

final_summary_df <- extracted_metrics_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  group_by(nh, piP, region, metric_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_value = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    # Transformations
    transformed_value = case_when(
      metric_type == "log_bias_rate" ~ (exp(avg_value) - 1) * 100,
      metric_type %in% c("abs_err_rate", "abs_err_prob") ~ avg_value,
      TRUE ~ avg_value
    ),
    # Map nh to Number of Hospitals
    n_hospitals = case_when(
      nh == 0 ~ 4, nh == 1 ~ 7, nh == 2 ~ 14, nh == 3 ~ 21, TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    # Clean labels
    display_metric = case_when(
      metric_type == "log_bias_rate" ~ "Percent Bias (Rate)",
      metric_type == "prob_bias" ~ "Bias (Prob)",
      metric_type == "abs_err_rate" ~ "MAE (Rate)",
      metric_type == "abs_err_prob" ~ "MAE (Prob)",
      metric_type == "cover_rate" ~ "Coverage (Rate)",
      metric_type == "cover_prob" ~ "Coverage (Prob)",
      TRUE ~ metric_type
    )
  )

# --- 3. GENERATING SEPARATE PLOTS ---

# Get list of unique metrics to loop over
# ... (Assume Steps 1 & 2 from the previous code are already run) ...

# --- 3. GENERATING SEPARATE PLOTS (2x2 Grid) ---

# Get list of unique metrics to loop over
unique_metrics <- unique(final_summary_df$display_metric)

# Create a named list to store the plots
all_plots <- list()

for (metric_name in unique_metrics) {
  
  # Filter data for this specific metric
  plot_data <- final_summary_df %>% 
    filter(display_metric == metric_name)
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = n_hospitals, y = transformed_value, color = region)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    # CHANGED: ncol = 2 creates the 2x2 grid for the 4 piP values
    facet_wrap(~ factor(piP), ncol = 2, labeller = as_labeller(function(x) paste("Average capture probability:", x))) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(4, 7, 14, 21)) +
    scale_color_viridis_d(name = "Health Region") +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(
      title = metric_name,
      x = "Number of Hospitals",
      y = "Value"
    ) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
    )
  
  
    # Save to list using the metric name
    
  all_plots[[metric_name]] <- p
}

```


## Parameter evaluation rates averaged

```{r param_avg_rate}

# --- 2. AGGREGATION AND TRANSFORMATION (Averaging across Regions) ---

# We assume 'extracted_metrics_list' exists from the previous step.
# If not, run Step 1 from the previous code block first.

extracted_metrics_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract parameters
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    # --- EXCLUSIONS ---
    if (is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    # 1. Convergence (Rhat > 1.1)
    if (is.null(sim$rhat) || any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    # 2. ES Validity (NA)
    if (is.null(sim$es_2024) || is.na(sim$es_2024)) return(NULL)
    # 3. Dataframe existence
    if (is.null(sim$param_metrics) || !is.data.frame(sim$param_metrics)) return(NULL)
    
    # --- EXTRACTION ---
    sim$param_metrics %>%
      dplyr::select(region, log_bias_rate, prob_bias, abs_err_rate, abs_err_prob, cover_rate, cover_prob) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = -region, names_to = "metric_type", values_to = "value") %>%
      mutate(sdalpha0 = sdalpha0, piP = piP)
  })
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

regional_summary_df <- extracted_metrics_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  
  # 1. Filter for only the RATE metrics of interest
  filter(metric_type %in% c("log_bias_rate", "abs_err_rate", "cover_rate")) %>%
  
  # 2. Group ONLY by experimental params (sdalpha0, piP) and metric type.
  # By OMITTING 'region', the summarise step averages across all 7 regions.
  group_by(sdalpha0, piP, metric_type) %>%
  summarise(
    global_avg = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Transformations
  mutate(
    transformed_value = case_when(
      metric_type == "log_bias_rate" ~ (exp(global_avg) - 1) * 100, # Percent Bias
      metric_type == "abs_err_rate" ~ sqrt(global_avg),                 # RMSE
      TRUE ~ global_avg                                             # Coverage stays as is
    ),
    
    # Clean labels for the facets
    display_metric = case_when(
      metric_type == "log_bias_rate" ~ "Bias",
      metric_type == "abs_err_rate" ~ "MAE",
      metric_type == "cover_rate" ~ "Coverage"
    )
  )

# --- 3. VISUALIZATION ---

# Set order of facets for visual consistency
regional_summary_df$display_metric <- factor(
  regional_summary_df$display_metric, 
  levels = c("Bias", "MAE", "Coverage")
)

ggplot(regional_summary_df, aes(x = sdalpha0, y = transformed_value, color = factor(piP))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  
  # Facet by the 3 Metrics
  facet_wrap(~ display_metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  
  # Axis and Color settings
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0.5,1,1.5)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = TeX("logit$^{-1}(\\alpha_0)$")) +
  
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "SD for alpha_0",
    y = "Metric Value"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0) # Ensure x-axis labels are flat
  )

```

## Parameter evaluation probs averaged

```{r param_avg_prob}

library(tidyverse)
library(viridis)

# --- 2. AGGREGATION AND TRANSFORMATION (Averaging across Regions) ---

# We assume 'extracted_metrics_list' exists from the previous step.

extracted_metrics_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  # Extract parameters
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    # --- EXCLUSIONS ---
    if (is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    # 1. Convergence (Rhat > 1.1)
    if (is.null(sim$rhat) || any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    # 2. ES Validity (NA)
    if (is.null(sim$es_2024) || is.na(sim$es_2024)) return(NULL)
    # 3. Dataframe existence
    if (is.null(sim$param_metrics) || !is.data.frame(sim$param_metrics)) return(NULL)
    
    # --- EXTRACTION ---
    sim$param_metrics %>%
      dplyr::select(region, log_bias_rate, prob_bias, abs_err_rate, abs_err_prob, cover_rate, cover_prob) %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = -region, names_to = "metric_type", values_to = "value") %>%
      mutate(sdalpha0 = sdalpha0, piP = piP)
  })
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

regional_prob_summary_df <- extracted_metrics_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  
  # 1. Filter for only the PROBABILITY metrics of interest
  filter(metric_type %in% c("prob_bias", "abs_err_prob", "cover_prob")) %>%
  
  # 2. Group ONLY by experimental params (sdalpha0, piP) and metric type
  group_by(sdalpha0, piP, metric_type) %>%
  summarise(
    global_avg = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Transformations
  mutate(
    transformed_value = case_when(
      # Apply square root to averaged MSE to get RMSE
      metric_type == "abs_err_prob" ~ sqrt(global_avg), 
      TRUE ~ global_avg # Bias and Coverage stay as the direct mean
    ),
    
    # Clean labels for the facets
    display_metric = case_when(
      metric_type == "prob_bias" ~ "Bias",
      metric_type == "abs_err_prob" ~ "MAE",
      metric_type == "cover_prob" ~ "Coverage"
    )
  )

# --- 3. VISUALIZATION ---

# Set order of facets for visual consistency
regional_prob_summary_df$display_metric <- factor(
  regional_prob_summary_df$display_metric, 
  levels = c("Bias", "MAE", "Coverage")
)

ggplot(regional_prob_summary_df, aes(x = sdalpha0, y = transformed_value, color = factor(piP))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  
  # Facet by the 3 Probability Metrics
  facet_wrap(~ display_metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  
  # Axis and Color settings
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0.5,1,1.5)) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(name = TeX("logit$^{-1}(\\alpha_0)$")) +
  
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = TeX("SD of normal prior for logit$(\\alpha_0)$"),
    y = "Metric Value"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )
```

## Create plot showing results for each simulation

```{r detail}
library(tidyverse)
library(parallel)
library(ggh4x) 

# --- 1. DATA EXTRACTION (Same as before) ---

raw_data_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    if (is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    if (is.null(sim$rhat) || any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    if (is.null(sim$es_2024) || is.na(sim$es_2024)) return(NULL)
    if (is.null(sim$param_metrics) || !is.data.frame(sim$param_metrics)) return(NULL)
    
    sim$param_metrics %>%
      dplyr::select(region, prob_bias, post_mean_prob, lb_prob, ub_prob) %>%
      mutate(sdalpha0 = sdalpha0, piP = piP)
  })
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# --- 2. DATA PROCESSING ---

full_df <- raw_data_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  mutate(region = str_sub(region, 1, 3)) %>% 
  group_by(sdalpha0, piP, region) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 100) %>%
  mutate(simNumber = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    implied_truth = post_mean_prob - prob_bias,
    ymin_bias = lb_prob - implied_truth,
    ymax_bias = ub_prob - implied_truth,
    piP_label = paste0("Prob: ", piP)
  )

# --- 3. UPDATED PLOTTING FUNCTION ---

create_forest_plot <- function(data, y_limits, y_breaks = waiver()) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = simNumber, y = prob_bias, ymin = ymin_bias, ymax = ymax_bias)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = 'red', linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_errorbar(width = 0, linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) + 
    geom_point(size = 0.5) +
    
    # CHANGED: Removed scales = "free_y" to keep scales consistent
    facet_nested(region ~ sdalpha0 + piP_label) +
    
    # CHANGED: Added explicit scale control with 'oob = scales::squish'
    # oob = scales::squish forces points outside the limits to be plotted at the limit edge
    # or simply allows the error bars to exist even if the bound is cut off. 
    # If you prefer them to disappear completely, remove 'oob = scales::squish'.
    scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, breaks = y_breaks) +
    
    labs(
      x = 'Simulation Number', 
      y = 'Bias (Probability Scale)', 
      title = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() + 
    theme(
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}

# --- 4. GENERATE THE TWO PLOTS WITH CUSTOM SCALES ---

# Dataset 1: Low Probability (0.2, 0.4)
df_low <- full_df %>% 
  filter(piP %in% c(0.1, 0.25))

# Low Plot: -0.25 to 0.5 (Breaks automatic unless specified)
plot_low <- create_forest_plot(
  df_low, 
  y_limits = c(-0.25, 0.5)
)

# Dataset 2: High Probability (0.6, 0.8)
df_high <- full_df %>% 
  filter(piP %in% c(0.5, 0.75))

# High Plot: -0.6 to 0.3 by 0.3
plot_high <- create_forest_plot(
  df_high, 
  y_limits = c(-0.4, 0.4),
  y_breaks = seq(-0.4, 0.4, by = 0.2)
)

# --- 5. VIEW PLOTS ---
# print(plot_low)
# print(plot_high)


```

## Forest plot for rates

```{r detail}

# --- 1. DATA EXTRACTION (Adapted for Rates) ---

raw_data_list <- mclapply(set, function(i) {
  x <- file_list[i]
  fname <- basename(x)
  
  sdalpha0 <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=sdalpha0_)\\d+(\\.\\d+)?"))
  piP <- as.numeric(str_extract(fname, "(?<=piP_)\\d*\\.?\\d+"))
  
  sim_list <- readRDS(x)
  
  map_dfr(sim_list, function(sim) {
    # --- EXCLUSIONS ---
    if (is.null(sim)) return(NULL)
    if (is.null(sim$rhat) || any(sim$rhat > 1.1, na.rm = TRUE)) return(NULL)
    if (is.null(sim$es_2024) || is.na(sim$es_2024)) return(NULL)
    if (is.null(sim$param_metrics) || !is.data.frame(sim$param_metrics)) return(NULL)
    
    # --- EXTRACTION (Using Rate Variables) ---
    sim$param_metrics %>%
      dplyr::select(
        region, 
        rate_bias,         # Changed from prob_bias
        post_mean_rate,    # Changed from post_mean_prob
        lb_mean_rate,      # Changed from lb_prob
        ub_mean_rate       # Changed from ub_prob
      ) %>%
      mutate(sdalpha0 = sdalpha0, piP = piP)
  })
}, mc.cores = num_cores)

# --- 2. DATA PROCESSING ---

full_df <- raw_data_list %>%
  bind_rows() %>%
  # Truncate region names
  mutate(region = str_sub(region, 1, 3)) %>% 
  group_by(sdalpha0, piP, region) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 100) %>%
  mutate(simNumber = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    # 1. Calculate the "True" value implicitly used in the simulation
    # Logic: Bias = Estimate - Truth  =>  Truth = Estimate - Bias
    implied_truth = post_mean_rate - rate_bias,
    
    # 2. Adjust Intervals to be on the Bias Scale (Relative to Zero)
    ymin_bias = lb_mean_rate - implied_truth,
    ymax_bias = ub_mean_rate - implied_truth,
    
    piP_label = paste0("Prob: ", piP)
  )

# --- 3. PLOTTING FUNCTION ---

create_forest_plot <- function(data, y_limits = NULL, y_breaks = waiver()) {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = simNumber, y = rate_bias, ymin = ymin_bias, ymax = ymax_bias)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = 'red', linetype = 2, linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_errorbar(width = 0, linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) + 
    geom_point(size = 0.5) +
    
    facet_nested(region ~ sdalpha0 + piP_label) +
    
    # scale_y_continuous will handle the limits. 
    # If y_limits is NULL (the default), it autoscales.
    scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits, breaks = y_breaks, oob = scales::squish) +
    
    labs(
      x = 'Simulation Number', 
      y = 'Bias (Rate Scale)', 
      title = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() + 
    theme(
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = 'white'),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}

# --- 4. GENERATE THE TWO PLOTS ---

# Dataset 1: Low Probability (0.2, 0.4)
df_low <- full_df %>% 
  filter(piP %in% c(0.1, 0.25))

# NOTE: I set limits to NULL to autoscale first. 
# You can inspect the plot and then add e.g., y_limits = c(-10, 10)
plot_low_rate <- create_forest_plot(
  df_low, 
  y_limits = c(-50,50) 
)

# Dataset 2: High Probability (0.6, 0.8)
df_high <- full_df %>% 
  filter(piP %in% c(0.5, 0.75))

plot_high_rate <- create_forest_plot(
  df_high, 
  y_limits = c(-25,25)
)

# --- 5. VIEW PLOTS ---
# print(plot_low_rate)
# print(plot_high_rate)


```
